full-width.heading: Full Width Section
full-width.text: This layout gives you the ability to put the main content to the left

full-width-card.heading: Full Width Section - Width Cards
full-width-card.text: Full width content with sub components in slot.

split.heading: Split Section
split.text: This layout gives you the ability to put the main content to the left

reversed.heading: Reversed Split Section
reversed.text: This layout gives you the ability to put the main content to the left

featured.heading: Featured Section
featured.text: This layout gives you the ability to put the main content to the left

stacked.heading: Stacked Split Section
stacked.text: Demonstrates how a stacked array of sections look like.

card.heading: Card Section
card.subHeading: A flexible card that transforms to a section.
card.text: This is a card that adapts to the width of the viewport.

with-buttons.heading: Segment with Call-to-Action Buttons
with-buttons.text: Demonstrates rendering a full-width segment with two call-to-action buttons placed beneath the content.

with-meta.heading: Segment with Date, Meta and Tags
with-meta.text: Shows a split segment using date, meta label, and tags for article or event-style layouts.

with-icon.heading: Segment with Icon
with-icon.text: Demonstrates the icon and iconBackgroundColor parameters displayed in a featured layout segment.

text-large.heading: Large Text Segment
text-large.text: Shows the textSize large variant which increases the content font size on medium and larger screens.

light-overlay.heading: Full Width with Light Overlay
light-overlay.text: Demonstrates the light overlay variant which applies a subtle brightness filter to the background image.

no-padding.heading: Segment Without Padding
no-padding.text: Shows paddingTop and paddingBottom set to false, removing vertical spacing from a split segment.

stretch.heading: Stretched Segment
stretch.text: Demonstrates the stretch parameter which bleeds the segment to full viewport width, breaking out of its surrounding container.
